questions.context:
A cuboid has length 8 cm, width 6 cm and height 4 cm.
ABCD is the base with AB = 8 cm and BC = 6 cm.
EFGH is the top face, directly above ABCD.

parts.label: a
parts.questionText: Calculate the length of the diagonal AC of the base.

parts.label: b
parts.questionText: Calculate the length of the space diagonal AG.

parts.label: c
parts.questionText: Calculate the angle that AG makes with the base ABCD.

markScheme.desc: Identifies the right triangle ACG with height CG = 4, base AC = 10
tan θ = 4/10 or sin θ = 4/10.77 oe

workingSteps: The angle is between AG and AC (the projection on the base)

questions.context:
A square-based pyramid VABCD has base side 12 cm and vertical height 8 cm.
V is the apex directly above the centre O of the square base.
M is the midpoint of AB.

parts.label: a
workingSteps: OM = half the side length = 12/2 = 6 cm

parts.label: b
parts.questionText: Calculate the slant height VM (the distance from V to the midpoint of AB).

parts.label: c
parts.questionText: Calculate the slant height VA (the distance from V to a corner of the base).

parts.label: d
parts.questionText: Calculate the angle that the triangular face VAB makes with the base.

markScheme.desc: Angle is between VM and OM at M

workingSteps: The angle between face VAB and the base is the angle VOM
In right triangle VOM: tan(VOM) = VO/OM = 8/6

questions.questionText:
A cone has base radius 5 cm and slant height 13 cm.
Calculate the vertical height of the cone.
questions.explanation: The vertical height, radius and slant height form a right-angled triangle.

questions.questionText:
A room is 5 m long, 4 m wide and 3 m high.
A spider is at the bottom corner of one end wall.
A fly is at the top corner of the opposite end wall (diagonally opposite).
Calculate the shortest distance the spider must walk along the walls to reach the fly.
(Hint: unfold the room.)

markScheme.desc: Unfolds floor and end wall: width = 5 + 3 = 8, or 4 + 3 = 7
Tries d² = 7² + 5² = 74 or d² = 4² + 8² = 80 oe (multiple options)
Minimum is √74 = 8.60 m (via floor+end) — accept any valid shortest path

workingSteps: Unfold option 1: floor + far wall → rectangle (4+3) by 5 = 7 × 5
Unfold option 2: floor + side wall → rectangle 4 by (5+3) = 4 × 8
questions.explanation: Unfold walls flat and use Pythagoras on the 2D net. Check multiple unfoldings.
